feature.name: js-profiling in dedicated workers
feature.creator: monicach@microsoft.com
feature.summary:
Allows js-profiling in dedicated workers

This feature enables the JavaScript Self‑Profiling (js-profiling) API in Dedicated Workers, while remaining gated by Document Policy. It allows developers to obtain low‑overhead CPU attribution for JavaScript execution in workers, with Document Policy support for workers tracked separately.

release.finding: Shipped in Chrome 147 (In developer trial (Behind a flag)).

adoption.finding: The WebDX/WebFeature counter 'Profiler' (ChromeStatus timeline, bucket_id 278) measured 0.0541% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-20. Tracked since 2025-01-09 with 548 data points. Mapping confidence: declared-family.
adoption.limitations: Counter measures the Profiler family/property, not this specific feature. Does not isolate the Chrome 147 increment.

deprecation.finding: This is a capability addition or behavior change, not a deprecation.
